flashcards.Back: A technology that allows multiple physical Ethernet links between two switches to be bundled into a single logical link, increasing bandwidth and providing redundancy.
The logical interface created when physical ports are aggregated into an EtherChannel. Configuration applied to this interface is replicated to all bundled physical ports.
The IEEE standard (802.3ad) for dynamic EtherChannel negotiation. it allows switches from different vendors to form a bundle automatically.
A Cisco-proprietary protocol for dynamic EtherChannel negotiation. it is the legacy predecessor to LACP.
An LACP negotiation mode where the port actively initiates the formation of an EtherChannel by sending LACP frames to its neighbor.
An LACP negotiation mode where the port only responds to LACP frames it receives but does not initiate negotiation itself.
A PAgP negotiation mode where the port actively attempts to negotiate the formation of an EtherChannel bundle.
A PAgP negotiation mode where the port waits passively for the neighbor to initiate negotiation.
A static configuration mode where the physical ports are forced to form an EtherChannel without any negotiation protocols (LACP or PAgP).
The interface-level command used to assign a physical port to an EtherChannel bundle and specify the mode (on, active, passive, etc.).
The mechanism that determines which physical link a frame will use. it uses a mathematical hash of header fields to distribute traffic across the bundle.
The global configuration command used to define the hashing algorithm used for traffic distribution across all EtherChannels on the switch.
A load balancing method (src-mac) where all frames from a specific host are always sent over the same physical link in the bundle.
A load balancing method (dst-ip) where traffic distribution is based on the destination network address, useful for traffic heading to multiple servers.
An EtherChannel bundle where the Port-Channel interface is configured as a routed port (using 'no switchport') and assigned an IP address.
The command used to convert a Layer 2 switch port or Port-Channel into a Layer 3 routed interface.
The primary verification command for EtherChannel. it shows the status of each bundle and indicates which physical ports are members.
A code seen in the 'summary' output indicating that the physical port is 'In Port-channel' and functioning correctly as part of the bundle.
A code seen in the 'summary' output indicating that the physical port is 'Down' or has been administratively disabled.
A verification command used to confirm which hashing method is currently active for traffic distribution across logical bundles.
An error state occurring when bundled physical ports have mismatched configurations (e.g., different VLANs or duplex settings), causing the bundle to fail.
The interaction where Spanning Tree treats the entire Port-Channel as a single interface, preventing loops while still allowing multiple physical links to be active.
A unique identifier assigned by LACP to each EtherChannel bundle to distinguish it from other bundles on the same switch.
A configurable value used in LACP to determine which switch has the authority to decide which ports are active in a bundle if more than 8 ports are configured.
The maximum number of active physical links supported in a single LACP EtherChannel, which is typically 8 on most Cisco platforms.
A configuration parameter that defines the minimum number of active physical links required for the logical Port-Channel interface to stay in an 'Up' state.
A diagnostic command used to predict which specific physical link a given packet flow (defined by MAC/IP) would take through a Port-Channel.
A value used to determine which physical ports should be placed in standby mode if the number of ports in an LACP bundle exceeds the maximum limit.
A feature that detects misconfigurations where a link is intended to be part of an EtherChannel but is not negotiating correctly, resulting in an err-disabled state.
The mathematical process that takes packet header info as input and produces an output value used to select one of the physical links in an EtherChannel.
